title: Introduction and Background
objective: Define the scope of the review on pedestrian crossing behavior research, establish the research problem domain, and outline the structure of the review

title: Research Methodologies in Pedestrian Crossing Studies
objective: Synthesize the methodological approaches employed across reviewed papers, examining the spectrum from empirical observation to advanced computational modeling

title: Microscopic Simulation and Behavioral Modeling Approaches
objective: Examine the development of pedestrian behavior models for micro-simulation, including gap acceptance criteria, social force models, and agent-based frameworks

title: Psychological and Attitudinal Models of Signal Non-Compliance
objective: Analyze theoretical frameworks including the Theory of Planned Behavior and structural equation models for predicting pedestrian violating behavior intentions

title: Mobile Phone Distraction and Visual Attention
objective: Review experimental studies on how mobile phone use affects pedestrian crossing behavior and visual attention allocation at signalized intersections

title: Geographic and Contextual Variation in Crossing Behavior
objective: Evaluate how pedestrian compliance and violation patterns vary across different geographic contexts, with particular attention to Chinese-city studies and mixed traffic conditions

title: Gap Analysis and Synthesis of Findings
objective: Identify unresolved claim differences across studies, synthesize conflicting and complementary findings, and assess the evidence base for key behavioral factors

title: Conclusions and Future Research Directions
objective: Summarize key insights, acknowledge limitations in the current evidence base, and propose future research priorities to address identified gaps
